summary: Deciduous molars have specific structural differences requiring careful restoration; thus, their proximal wall cavities must converge towards the occlusal surface to protect fragile structures while allowing efficient filling. Key factors include managing reduced dental tissue strength and facilitating potential future treatments like crowns. To summarize concisely yet comprehensively based solely upon provided context: When addressing Class II cavities in deciduous molars, it is essential they exhibit proximal walls angling inward near the top due primarily to weakened enamel/dentin necessitating minimal invasive treatment approaches alongside preparation needs compatible with subsequent care requirements including possible capping later if needed. Sentence count maintained at two per instruction request above.
